mechanism_failure.name: The Broken Mechanism
mechanism_failure.summary: The lighthouse mechanism is in pieces. Someone — or something — took it apart deliberately.

prior_keepers.name: The Lost Keepers
prior_keepers.summary: Three Keepers before you. None left the island. Their echoes remain.

the_warden.name: What Lies Beneath
the_warden.summary: The lighthouse was not built to guide ships.

marens_secret.name: The Archivist's Burden
marens_secret.summary: Maren knows more than she admits. She has been here through more loops than you.

the_seal.name: The Old Seal
the_seal.summary: Something was bound beneath the island long before the lighthouse was built.

blacksmith_lying.name: The Blacksmith Arrived Late
blacksmith_lying.summary: Tobias claims he arrived before the storm. The harbour records disagree.

chapel_symbol.name: The Chapel Symbol
chapel_symbol.summary: The symbol above the chapel door is not a religious icon. It is a containment mark.

light_source_truth.title: The Beam Does Not Warn. It Calls.
light_source_truth.description: Something unnatural feeds on the lighthouse beam. Find out what.

step.talk_to_vael: Find the one who walks in the water.

archivist_bargain.title: What the Archive Remembers
archivist_bargain.description: Maren wants archive pages. The question is what she'll give in return.

step.find_archive_page: Find a page in the archive basement.
step.return_to_maren: Return to Maren with what you found.

harbor_silence.title: Why the Ships Don't Come
harbor_silence.description: The harbour has been closed for months. Silas knows more than he admits.

step.talk_to_elara: Find someone else who knows about the harbour.
step.confront_silas: Confront Silas with what you've learned.

vael_origin.title: Echoes in the Deep
vael_origin.description: Something ancient stirs beneath the island. Vael knows what it is — but will not say freely.

step.examine_symbols: Examine the bioluminescent symbols in the tidal caves.
step.examine_binding: Study the binding rune in the mechanism room.
step.talk_vael_again: Return to Vael and ask about what you've found.

keeper_betrayal.title: The Last Entry
keeper_betrayal.description: The previous Keeper's final log holds a secret the island has been protecting for decades.

step.talk_maren: Ask Maren what she knows about the previous Keeper.
step.examine_ledger: Find the sealed ledger in the archive basement.

spirit_binding.title: Chains Beneath the Stone
spirit_binding.description: The mechanism's symbols are not decorative. They are a record of a crime.

step.examine_binding_rune: Examine the binding rune in the mechanism room.
step.examine_hidden_names: Find the hidden names in the chapel.
step.talk_elara: Talk to Elara about what the names mean.

island_history.title: What the Archives Forgot
island_history.description: The archive's gaps are not accidental. Someone decided what the island would remember.

step.examine_portrait: Examine the burned portrait in the ruins.
step.examine_hidden_names: Find the hidden names in the chapel.
step.talk_maren: Return to Maren with what you've found.

final_signal.title: The Last Frequency
final_signal.description: You have gathered what there is to know. The lighthouse has been waiting for this moment since before you arrived.

step.visit_top_night: Ascend to the lighthouse top while it burns.
step.talk_vael_tier3: Find Vael and speak with them one final time.

vael_origin.title: The Vael's True Nature
vael_origin.desc: Not a monster. An ancient intelligence, wrongly imprisoned within the mechanism a century ago by keepers who feared what they did not understand.

mechanism_purpose.title: The Mechanism's Purpose
mechanism_purpose.desc: The lighthouse does not merely guide ships. The mechanism beneath it is a binding engine — it exists to trap, not illuminate.

light_source_truth.title: The Light's Source
light_source_truth.desc: The lighthouse burns on no oil, no gas. It feeds on something else entirely. The lens inscription names it: 'borrowed life.'

keeper_betrayal.title: The Keeper's Fate
keeper_betrayal.desc: The previous Keeper discovered the truth and tried to leave. They never reached the mainland. The last log entry is dated three weeks before anyone reported them missing.

spirit_binding.title: The Binding Ritual
spirit_binding.desc: The runes in the mechanism room are not decorative. They are a cage. Whoever conducted the original binding knew exactly what they were doing — and chose to do it anyway.

island_history.title: What This Island Was
island_history.desc: Before the lighthouse, before the village, there was a place of convergence. The Vael was not summoned here — it was born here. The island was built around it.

final_signal.title: The Final Transmission
final_signal.desc: On the night the mechanism fails completely, the lighthouse sends one last signal. Not to ships. To something much further away. You have seen it. You cannot unsee it.

keepers_cottage.enter: The cottage smells of salt and old paper. A journal sits on the table — not mine. The handwriting is steady until the last entry, where it becomes a series of crossed-out lines and then, finally, a single sentence I don't understand yet. I copy it down. I have a feeling I'll need it.

lighthouse_base.enter: The lighthouse rises above me, taller than it looks from the village. The door is unlocked, which surprises me — I expected resistance, a lock, a reason not to enter. The only resistance is the threshold itself: I stand here for a moment longer than I need to. Something about crossing this line feels significant. I cross it.

lighthouse_top.enter: The view from here changes everything. I can see the whole island laid out below me like a map of itself — every path, every building, every dark patch of water at the cliff base. From up here, it looks deliberate. It looks like it was designed to be seen this way. I write down what I can see. I'm not sure what I'm looking for, but I think I'll know it when I find it.

harbor.enter: The harbour smells of salt water and something else — oil, or old rope, or the particular smell of a working place that has stopped working. The boats are still here. The people are not, mostly. One lantern burns at the harbourmaster's window. I note the time. I note that the harbour is quieter than a harbour should be.

village_square.enter: The square is the heart of the village, and it feels hollow. People pass through it quickly, without stopping, without meeting eyes. I stand in the centre for a while, trying to read the space. The notice board draws my attention first. Then the well. Something about the well is wrong, and I find myself walking toward it before I've decided to.

ruins.enter: The ruins are older than anything else on this island. I can feel it in the stones — not dramatically, not in any way I can explain, just the specific weight of something that has been standing longer than any living person has been alive. The stones don't fit together right; they're not the style or scale of any building I know. Someone built this for a reason I don't have a word for yet.

mechanism_room.enter: Gears the size of millstones, connected by shafts that disappear into the floor and the ceiling. Whatever drove this mechanism once, it wasn't wind or water — there's no intake, no channel, no means of external power I can identify. The room smells of metal and something else, something older. The walls are covered in symbols. I start drawing them. It will take several visits to get them all.

chapel.enter: St. Maret's Chapel: doors open, candles lit, no one in attendance. The candles are fresh. Someone lit them today, perhaps this morning. I sit for a while in one of the pews and try to think, but the chapel doesn't allow neutral thinking — there's too much of it, pressing in, the weight of what happened here and what didn't. I notice, when I finally look, that my name is on the wall.

mill.enter: The old mill creaks in a wind that isn't there. I stop and listen to be sure: the air is still, the trees at the forest's edge are not moving, but the mill groans and settles as if under pressure from something I can't see. The wheel hasn't turned in years — the axle is rusted, the paddles warped. Something else is turning. I write this down before I can talk myself out of it.

forest_path.enter: The path begins at the village edge and leads into a treeline that should, by my count, only take ten minutes to cross. It always takes longer than that. The path itself seems cooperative — it goes where I need to go — but the relationship between distance walked and distance covered is unreliable here. I've stopped trying to track it precisely. I mark where I notice things instead.

cliffside.enter: The cliff edge drops sharply, the cove far below glittering in whatever light is available. I keep a careful distance from the edge — not from fear exactly, more from respect for a place that has claimed things. At certain hours, in certain light, something moves in the water far below. I've stopped pretending it's a seal. I watch it. It has not yet looked back, but I have the impression it's aware that I'm here.

archive_basement.enter: Row upon row of stone shelves, catalogued in a system I don't fully understand, holding documents in materials that range from paper to vellum to something that might be cured skin. The smell is overwhelming at first — age, damp, the specific musk of things that have not been disturbed in a long time. I spend the first few minutes just reading labels. Whatever is in this archive, it predates everything I thought I knew about this place.

tidal_caves.enter: The tide has retreated far enough to allow entry — I'll have perhaps two hours before it comes back. The walls of the cave are covered in writing, both carved and what appears to be bioluminescent growth, both glowing faintly in the dark. I'm not sure which came first. I bring my notebook and begin at the entrance, working inward. I'm aware that the light at the back of the cave is not reflected from outside. It has its own source.
